question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getNodes() uses Schedulers.boundedElastic() for pagination retries, and by opening many concurrent streams with slow consumption (back-pressure), exhaust the bounded elastic thread pool and prevent the mirror node from completing address book pagination for legitimate clients needing gossip node information?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user call getNodes() and receive nodeAddress.setNodeId() for every node, correlating node IDs with their service endpoints to build a precise targeting list, then use that list to send crafted malformed gossip messages to specific node IDs to disrupt their gossip participation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the jitter(0.5) and withFixedDelay(addressBookProperties.getPageDelay()) in the RepeatSpec to predict retry timing and synchronize their own request flood with the mirror node's internal retry cycles, amplifying resource contention and preventing gossip transaction data from being served?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send an AddressBookQuery with a fileId whose shard, realm, or num fields are set to boundary values (e.g., Long.MAX_VALUE), causing EntityId.of() to produce an unexpected EntityId that passes the null check but fails the validFileIds.contains() check in a way that reveals internal shard/realm configuration of the gossip network?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of per-client connection limits on the getNodes() gRPC endpoint to open the maximum number of HTTP/2 streams allowed by the server configuration, each streaming address book data, and use the resulting resource exhaustion to prevent the mirror node from accepting new connections from gossip-monitoring clients?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user retrieve the memo field via ByteString.copyFromUtf8(addressBookEntry.getMemo()) in toNodeAddress() and find sensitive operational metadata embedded in node memos that reveals gossip node operator identities or infrastructure details, enabling targeted social engineering or infrastructure attacks to suppress transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the address book is cached (ADDRESS_BOOK_ENTRY_CACHE) and send requests timed to coincide with cache expiry (cacheExpiry = 2 seconds), causing a cache stampede where many concurrent requests all miss the cache simultaneously and flood the database, disrupting gossip node data availability?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the streaming nature of getNodes() to perform a slow-read attack — consuming responses one byte at a time — keeping the server-side reactive subscription alive indefinitely and preventing the disposable from being cleaned up, leading to resource exhaustion that impacts gossip transaction processing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user use the complete node address information returned by toNodeAddress() (IP, port, cert hash, public key, stake, account ID) to identify the highest-stake gossip nodes and concentrate a DoS attack on those nodes, maximally disrupting transaction gossip with minimal attack resources?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getNodes() does not validate the gRPC request size, sending an AddressBookQuery with an extremely large protobuf payload (e.g., oversized fileId fields) to cause excessive memory allocation during deserialization and trigger an OOM condition that disrupts gossip-related services?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the subscribe(responseObserver::onNext, responseObserver::onError, responseObserver::onCompleted) pattern to trigger responseObserver::onError with a crafted exception that causes the gRPC framework to send an error response containing internal stack trace information, revealing gossip node infrastructure details?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: toIpAddressV4()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that toIpAddressV4() calls InetAddress.getByName() which may perform reverse DNS lookups, and by monitoring DNS query logs (if the attacker controls a DNS server that the mirror node queries), learn the internal network topology of gossip nodes and use that to plan targeted disruption?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the nodeStakeCacheExpiry of 24 hours in AddressBookProperties to obtain stale stake data via getNodes(), use that stale data to identify nodes that were previously high-stake but are now low-stake, and target those nodes based on outdated information to disrupt gossip?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getNodes() exposes the full address book including both file101 and file102 entries by making two separate requests with different fileId values, obtaining a complete dual-file view of all gossip nodes and using that comprehensive map to plan a network-wide gossip disruption attack?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux reactive pipeline in getNodes() to trigger a back-pressure overflow by consuming responses slower than they are produced, causing the Exceptions.isOverflow() check in ProtoUtil.toStatusRuntimeException() to fire and return DEADLINE_EXCEEDED with the OVERFLOW_ERROR message, which reveals that the server is under load and can be used to time further attacks?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user retrieve the setPort() value from each ServiceEndpoint via toNodeAddress() and use the port numbers to identify non-standard gossip ports that may have weaker firewall rules, enabling targeted port-level attacks to disrupt gossip communication for specific transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the transactionOperations.execute() wrapping in NetworkServiceImpl.page() to cause database transaction timeouts by holding the gRPC stream open while consuming data slowly, leading to TransientDataAccessException that maps to RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED status and reveals database connection pool limits?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send repeated getNodes() requests with alternating valid fileId values (file101 and file102) to continuously invalidate and repopulate the ADDRESS_BOOK_ENTRY_CACHE, causing a cache thrashing condition that degrades mirror node performance and prevents timely gossip node data from being served?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: toNodeAddress()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the @SuppressWarnings('deprecation') annotation on toNodeAddress() to infer that deprecated protobuf fields (like setMemo with ByteString) are still populated, and use those deprecated fields to extract additional node metadata not intended for public consumption that could aid in targeting gossip nodes?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of IP-based rate limiting on the getNodes() gRPC endpoint to use a botnet of IP addresses to each open a single getNodes() stream, collectively exhausting the mirror node's connection pool and preventing legitimate gossip transaction monitoring?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getNodes() logs 'Querying for address book: {}' with the full filter object via doOnSubscribe(), and if log aggregation is accessible, use those logs to correlate request patterns and identify when the address book is being updated, timing attacks to coincide with address book transitions to disrupt gossip?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the pageSize=10 default in AddressBookProperties to infer the exact number of gossip nodes by counting the number of pages returned in a getNodes() stream, using that count to determine the minimum number of nodes that need to be disrupted to break gossip quorum?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: toFilter()] Can an unprivileged external user send an AddressBookQuery with a fileId set to a valid but non-address-book entity ID (e.g., a topic or account ID), causing EntityId.of() to succeed but the validFileIds.contains() check to fail, and use the resulting INVALID_ARGUMENT error message to enumerate which entity IDs are considered valid address book files?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the UnsafeByteOperations.unsafeWrap() call in ProtoUtil.toByteString() to cause memory corruption if the underlying byte array is modified concurrently, leading to incorrect node certificate hash or IP address data being returned to clients and causing them to connect to wrong gossip endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/controller/NetworkController.java -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: getNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getNodes() does not enforce any maximum streaming duration, keeping a gRPC stream open for hours by setting limit=0 and consuming responses slowly, tying up server resources and preventing the mirror node from serving gossip node data to other clients?
